more than its radius. If the curved surface area

of the cylinder is ‘672π’ cm , then find height of

the cylinder.

the cylinder.
questionHtml:
The height of a right circular cylinder is 16 cm

more than its radius. If the curved surface area

of the cylinder is ‘672π’ cm , then find height of

the cylinder.
explanation:
Let the radius of the cylinder be ‘x’ cm

So, height of the cylinder = ‘x + 16’ cm

Curved surface area of cylinder = 2πrh {Where

‘r’ is radius and ‘h’ is height}

So, 672π = 2π 
 x 
 (x + 16)

Or, 336 = x + 16x

So, x + 16x – 336 = 0

Or, x + 28x – 12x – 336 = 0

Or, x(x – 12) + 28(x – 12) = 0

Or, (x – 12)(x + 28) = 0

So, x = 12 or x = -28

Since the radius cannot be negative, we may

discard x = -28.

So, height of the cylinder = 12 + 16 = 28 cm

Hence, option c.
